title: BCM Solar System Batch — Scale-Invariance Test
author: Stephen Justin Burdick Sr. — Emerald Entities LLC

Mercury.bcm_class: Class III analog — extinct or minimal substrate
Mercury.notes: Slow rotation, low flux. Near-zero substrate injection.

Venus.bcm_class: Class III analog — no intrinsic induction
Venus.notes: Zero B field. Substrate interaction via ionospheric coupling only. Retrograde rotation.

Earth.bcm_class: Class I analog — dipole lock m=1
Earth.notes: High-stability dipole. Substrate funded by core dynamo. Biosphere substrate interaction hypothesis open.

Mars.bcm_class: Class II analog — residual substrate memory
Mars.notes: Dead dynamo. Crustal remanent fields = ρ_initial ≠ 0. Fossil substrate from active epoch.

Jupiter.bcm_class: Class I — high energy substrate pump
Jupiter.notes: Strongest dynamo in solar system. m=1 dominant. Candidate for full substrate scale-invariance test.

Saturn.bcm_class: Primary test case — m=6 hexagonal geometric lock
Saturn.notes: Confirmed m=6 standing wave. λ_planetary=0.082 vs λ_galactic=0.1. 18% delta — scale invariance candidate.

Uranus.bcm_class: Class V-A analog — asymmetric off-center coupling
Uranus.notes: Retrograde rotation. Highly tilted magnetic axis (59°). Off-center substrate coupling. Low σ vs metallic H.

Neptune.bcm_class: Class V-A analog — high-shear asymmetric substrate
Neptune.notes: Highly asymmetric field (47° tilt, offset 0.55 R_N). Great Dark Spot analog. High shear substrate pump.
